error_signature: Execute Command node is disabled by default
title: Execute Command node disabled in n8n v2
symptom: Users trying to delete files using the Execute Command node in n8n v2 encounter issues because the node is disabled by default.
applies_when: Trying to use Execute Command node
root_cause: In n8n v2.x, the Execute Command node is disabled by default for security reasons, which prevents users from executing shell commands directly within workflows.
fix_steps: Use the command 'rm -f /tmp/*' in the Execute Command node to delete files.
agent_instruction: Enable the Execute Command node and use it to run the delete command for the specified folder.
safety_notes: Be cautious with the 'rm -f' command as it will permanently delete files without confirmation.
